raw_answers:
Q001:Character types: The password must contain at least one uppercase letter, one lowercase letter, one numeric digit, and one special character (e.g., @, #, $, %, ^, &, *). Restrictions: Consecutive spaces are forbidden. The password cannot contain the user's explicit email prefix or username to mitigate easy guessing.
Q003: Email format must contain @ and domain
Q002: meaningful inline error
Q004: The system will send a unique verification link via email immediately after registration. This link is valid for 24 hours. Users can request a resend of the code every 60 seconds.
Q005: 8 ~ 12 characters allowed

answers.Q001: Character types: The password must contain at least one uppercase letter, one lowercase letter, one numeric digit, and one special character (e.g., @, #, $, %, ^, &, *). Restrictions: Consecutive spaces are forbidden. The password cannot contain the user's explicit email prefix or username to mitigate easy guessing.
answers.Q003: Email format must contain @ and domain
answers.Q004: The system will send a unique verification link via email immediately after registration. This link is valid for 24 hours. Users can request a resend of the code every 60 seconds.

answered_clarifications.question: What is the password complexity requirement (e.g., must include uppercase, lowercase, digit, special character)?
answered_clarifications.reason: Password complexity affects security and test coverage for valid/invalid passwords.
answered_clarifications.answer: Character types: The password must contain at least one uppercase letter, one lowercase letter, one numeric digit, and one special character (e.g., @, #, $, %, ^, &, *). Restrictions: Consecutive spaces are forbidden. The password cannot contain the user's explicit email prefix or username to mitigate easy guessing.

answered_clarifications.question: What is the exact email format validation rule (e.g., must contain '@' and a domain)?
answered_clarifications.reason: Email format validation affects positive and negative test cases.
answered_clarifications.answer: Email format must contain @ and domain

answered_clarifications.question: What error message should be displayed when a user tries to register with an already existing email?
answered_clarifications.reason: Error messages are needed for UX and validation test cases.

answered_clarifications.question: What is the email verification mechanism (e.g., link sent via email, token expiry time)?
answered_clarifications.reason: Verification flow impacts test scenarios for successful and expired token handling.
answered_clarifications.answer: The system will send a unique verification link via email immediately after registration. This link is valid for 24 hours. Users can request a resend of the code every 60 seconds.

answered_clarifications.question: Are the password length boundaries inclusive (i.e., 8 and 12 characters allowed)?
answered_clarifications.reason: Boundary clarity is needed for exact test case design.
